name: brier
description: Transfer-learning genetic risk prediction via Bregman-divergence-based regularized integrative estimation. Local computation through your R installation.
long_description:
BRIER is a Bregman-divergence-based regularized integrative estimator for transfer-learning-based genetic risk prediction. It improves prediction in a target cohort by borrowing information from external cohorts, pretrained external models, or summary-level association statistics, under high-dimensional regularization (LASSO, MCP, SCAD).

This MCP extension exposes the BRIER fitting functions as Claude Desktop tools, plus a data-first guided wizard. Computation runs entirely on your local machine via your installed R; patient-level data never leaves the machine. Prerequisites (you must install these first): R >= 4.0 (https://cran.r-project.org/) and the BRIER R package (in R: remotes::install_github('UM-KevinHe/BRIER')). Python is NOT required; Claude Desktop's bundled uv runtime handles that automatically.

v0.7.0 reworks the wizard to be data-first: instead of interviewing the user about their problem, it asks for a data path and inspects the file directly, deriving most answers (outcome family, predictor type, data shape, sample sizes) heuristically. The user confirms or corrects the inspection summary in free text, then the wizard produces a recommendation grounded in what's actually in the data, with canonical_call already filled in using the user's R expression paths. Supports R objects (.rds / .rda / .RData), tabular files (.csv / .tsv / .txt / .xlsx), and genotype binaries (.pgen / .bed / .bgen, inspected via their companion files).

v2.0.0 broadens data inspection to those tabular and genotype-binary formats and adds support for agentic preprocessing: a containment profile for safely running an agent against your data, and a CLAUDE.md working guide that drives a staged, checkpointed workflow (inspect, clean, fit, evaluate, report) with a decision layer that compares baselines and proposes which external sources to keep.

rscript_path.title: Rscript executable
rscript_path.description: Full path to the Rscript executable on your machine. Prerequisites: (1) R >= 4.0 from https://cran.r-project.org/, and (2) the BRIER R package - in R, run remotes::install_github('UM-KevinHe/BRIER'). Typical Rscript locations - Windows: C:\Program Files\R\R-4.x.x\bin\Rscript.exe; macOS: /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Resources/bin/Rscript or /usr/local/bin/Rscript; Linux: /usr/bin/Rscript.
